concerns.text: FR-004 references a confidence‑interval level as `[deferred]`. The idea explicitly states a 95 % confidence level; without a concrete value the rule “reported p‑value lies outside the confidence interval derived from the reconstructed test (at [deferred] level)” is ambiguous and cannot be operationalised.

FR-005 mentions a Wilson `[deferred]` confidence interval but omits the confidence level (e.g., 95 %). The idea requires a 95 % Wilson CI; the placeholder prevents verification of the correct interval.

FR-005 adds several statistical analyses (chi‑squared goodness‑of‑fit test, Cochran‑Armitage trend test, Bonferroni‑adjusted subgroup tests) that are not present in the original idea. This constitutes scope over‑reach.

FR-001 (accept list of URLs) is not cited by any user story; the idea’s US‑1, US‑2, US‑3, US‑4 all reference other FRs but none explicitly anchor FR-001, breaking traceability and suggesting a mis‑aligned scope.
FR-002 (automatic extraction) is orphaned – no user story references it, whereas the idea’s US‑2 maps to FR‑002. The missing anchor obscures the intended functional coverage.
FR-003 (reconstruct p‑value) is not linked to any user story, despite the idea’s US‑2 explicitly requiring reconstruction of significance.
FR-006 (export reproducible research package) is not anchored to any user story; US‑3 describes reproducibility but does not cite FR‑006, creating a gap between the idea and the spec.

FR-009 (CPU‑only execution constraints) is orphaned – no user story explicitly references it, even though US‑4 mentions CI constraints. The lack of anchoring weakens the traceability of the CI requirement.

SC-002 (inconsistency‑detection precision ≥ 90 %) is not tied to any user story; the idea’s success criteria only mention precision in the context of US‑1/US‑2. The missing anchor makes it unclear which stakeholder the criterion serves.

SC-003 (all statistical tests must be computed with SciPy) lacks a user‑story anchor; the idea’s methodology sketch mentions SciPy but does not map this criterion to a specific story, breaking the traceability chain.

Beyond the extra analyses already flagged, FR-005 also includes “temporal trends … using a simple proportion‑difference test at α = 0.05” which the idea only alludes to in US‑2. The specification’s detailed sub‑analyses exceed the idea’s stated scope.
